chapter_title.en: Chapter on What the Pilgrim in the State of Ihram Must Avoid: Obscenity, Sin, and Disputation During Hajj

text: 2589 - وَ سَأَلَهُ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ وَاقَعَ اِمْرَأَتَهُ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «عَلَيْهِ جَزُورٌ كَوْمَاءُ» فَقَالَ لاَ يَقْدِرُ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «يَنْبَغِي لِأَصْحَابِهِ أَنْ يَجْمَعُوا لَهُ وَ لاَ يُفْسِدُوا عَلَيْهِ حَجَّهُ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2589 - Abu Basir asked Imam (as) about a man who had intercourse with his wife while in the state of Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "He must offer a large camel (jazur kawmā’)."
Abu Basir said: "What if he cannot afford it?"
Imam (as) replied: "It is upon his companions to contribute on his behalf, and they should not invalidate his Hajj."
